description:
## Goal

Wire the vector store and concept graph into a hybrid retrieval pipeline, connect it to a model-agnostic LLM provider, and enforce the mandated citation procedure end-to-end via a CLI.

## Architecture

- **`guru/retriever.py`** — `HybridRetriever` runs vector similarity (with user-prefs filters) in parallel with a concept graph walk (query → concepts → PARALLELS/CONTRASTS → EXPRESSES → chunks), then merges and re-ranks with a tradition-diversity boost and edge-tier weighting (verified 1.0× / proposed 0.7× / inferred 0.4×).
- **`guru/preferences.py`** — `UserPreferences` dataclass with whitelist/blacklist of traditions and texts; converts to vector-store filter clauses and chunk-level allow checks.
- **`guru/prompt.py`** — Formats retrieved chunks with full citation metadata and assembles the system prompt that enforces citation rules and edge-tier hedging language.
- **`guru/model.py`** — `ModelProvider` protocol with Ollama, Anthropic, and OpenAI implementations; selected via `config/model.toml`.
- **`guru/cli.py`** — `guru query "..."` entry point printing the citation-grounded response with a result-stats footer.

## Outputs

- All modules under `guru/`
- `config/model.toml`
- Three integration tests covering the full path

## Acceptance Criteria

- Cross-tradition queries return chunks from multiple traditions
- User-preference filters never leak excluded traditions into results or citations
- Every substantive paragraph contains at least one `[Tradition | Text | Section]` citation
- Hedging language is used for ◇ Proposed edges
- Agent explicitly states when no source supports a claim — never fabricates
- Provider swap (ollama ↔ anthropic ↔ openai) requires only a config change
- End-to-end query latency under 10s excluding model inference

## See Also

docs/guru-implementation.md §"Stage 5: Retrieval & Model Integration"
